| 053 | 2026-05-31 Phase-B BUILD subB adversary F1 (WARN) | **D-PHASE-B-EMIT-PERMANENT-FAILURE-SURFACE.** `emit_correction`'s blanket `except Exception` (correct for transient PG faults â€” best-effort آ§6.6) also swallows PERMANENT failures (malformed payload, serialization edge, future schema drift) with only a `logger.warning`. The آ§10.1 replay backstop can only replay rows that made it INTO `outbox_events` â€” a row whose INSERT never succeeded has zero durability. Fix: distinguish transient (pool/conn) from permanent failures; surface non-transient via a metric / error-log-at-ERROR rather than silent drop. Low risk today (payload shape is code-controlled, json.dumps(default=str)). **Empirically confirmed (session 75 smoke):** a non-UUID-coercible `aggregate_id` (a synthetic relation id) made `emit_correction` silently drop the correction (uuid.UUID() raised â†’ swallowed). Harmless in production (all real entity/relation/event ids are 32-hex â†’ UUID-coercible), but it is a concrete instance of the swallow-masks-permanent-failure concern. | Phase E hardening (when corrections feed gold) | LOW |
| 052 | 2026-05-31 Phase-B BUILD subA adversary F-A2 (WARN) | **D-PHASE-B-DIFFCLASS-DESC-GRANULARITY.** `short_description` is classed as CONTENT alongside name/aliases, so a description-only edit makes the content hash differ â†’ `diff_class="boundary"`. But `boundary` is meant for name/alias rename signal; glossary PATCH is *mostly* description edits, so `boundary` over-counts in `/corrections/stats` (feeds future eval-gold/few-shot tiers). Matches the locked design آ§3 (single content hash), so deferred not blocking. Fix: split a name/alias hash from the description hash (schema add) so `boundary` fires only on name/alias deltas; route description-only edits to `other`. Analytics-only, no write-path impact. | Phase D/E (when stats feed tiers) | LOW |

| 046 | 2026-05-31 Phase-B design آ§6.6 (cross-store) | **D-PHASE-B-CORRECTION-RECONCILE.** KS graph edits are Neo4j; the correction outbox row is Postgres â†’ no true transactional atomicity. | 018 | 2026-05-16 tilemap Phase 1 human-in-loop review HIGH-1 | `penrose::assign_zone_tiles` is O(tiles أ— vertices) â€” `nearest_vertex` linearly scans the full Penrose vertex field for every grid tile. Mitigated: `cmd_complete` defensively re-normalizes so the OUTPUT tag list is always comma-safe â€” only the state-file display value can be stale. Fix: normalize unconditionally at the top of `cmd_amaw_enable`, or normalize on every state read. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 001 | 2026-05-15 amaw-l3-deepen /review-impl #5 | `task_slug` not validated â€” comma in slug splits into multiple tags downstream (`",".join(tags)` then mcp-query.py splits back on comma). No injection risk (subprocess list form). Fix: normalize comma â†’ dash in `cmd_amaw_enable`, or argparse-validate slug pattern `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$`. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 002 | 2026-05-15 amaw-l3-deepen /review-impl #6 | `"REJECTED" in evidence.upper()` substring match in `cmd_complete` bridge logic â€” false positives on "NOT REJECTED" or "rejected the rejected pattern". Fix: word-boundary regex `\bREJECTED\b` or check evidence prefix `"REJECTED:"` / `"status: REJECTED"`. | L4 hardening | LOW |
| 004 | 2026-05-15 amaw-l3-deepen /review-impl #8 | `cmd_pre_commit` early-exits silent when no STATE_FILE, but `&&`-chained `cmd_amaw_pre_commit` then calls `load_state()` which CREATES `.workflow-state.json` from INITIAL_STATE. Side effect: every commit by an agent NOT in any tracked task creates a stale state file, surfacing confusing `[ ]` markers on next `status`. Fix: in `cmd_amaw_pre_commit`, also early-exit if STATE_FILE absent before `load_state()`. | L4 hardening | LOW |

### Lore-enrichment workstream deferrals (`LE-` prefix; merged 2026-06-01)
> Carried under `LE-` to avoid the numeric-ID collision with main's Phase-B/geo track (both branches independently allocated 044â€“055). Lore-enrichment's RESOLVED items (its old 044 KNOWLEDGE_SERVICE_URL wiring, 050 injection hardening, 052-for-P1 token metering) are NOT relisted â€” they're in git history + `docs/03_planning/lore-enrichment/SESSION_HANDOFF.md`.

| ID | Origin | Description | Target | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ~~LE-056~~ | 2026-05-30 lore-enrichment C15 WARN-3 | âœ… **RESOLVED 2026-06-01 (C2, post-main-merge re-decision).** Judge-diversity floor: `app/eval/judge_usefulness.py` `acceptable` now requires â‰¥2 judges from **â‰¥2 DISTINCT families** (`JudgeSpec.family`, default=label, normalized strip+casefold so case/whitespace can't fake diversity) **AND** خ؛ â‰¥ a configurable floor (`settings.judge_kappa_floor` / `LORE_ENRICHMENT_JUDGE_KAPPA_FLOOR`, default 0.0 = below-chance disqualifies; non-finite خ؛ treated as uncomputable). Two qwen near-clones no longer self-certify even at خ؛=1.0. Wired through `runner.py` + `gate.py` message; the C15 live-smoke now tags `family`. /review-impl fixed family-normalization + NaN-خ؛ guard. 549 pytest pass. **Residuals (accepted):** (a) خ؛=None (disjoint judge coverage â†’ no common item) does NOT disqualify â€” gate clears on family-count alone with agreement unmeasured (deliberate PO choice; tighten later if flaky-coverage runs slip through); (b) `JudgeUsefulnessResult.reasons` is not surfaced to the scorecard/gate message (observability only). | DONE | RESOLVED |
| ~~LE-051~~ | 2026-05-30 lore-enrichment C14-FIX (WARN-1) | âœ… **RESOLVED 2026-06-04 (verified token-safe; cursor = won't-fix micro-opt).** Re-reviewed `runner.run_job`: a gap in `skip_gap_refs` is skipped at runner.py:198 **BEFORE** the cost-charge AND the LLM `run_gap` (`outcome.resumed_skipped.append(...); continue`) â€” so a resumed job spends **neither budget nor tokens** on done gaps (the real concern). The resume worker passes ALL already-persisted `gap_ref`s, so done gaps never reach the reload/`deduped` path. The only thing NOT done is persisting a "remainder cursor" so the runner doesn't iterate-and-skip the done prefix â€” but that skip is pure in-memory iteration (no I/O, no LLM), negligible even for a 1000-gap job. **Won't-fix:** the micro-opt changes no correctness or cost and adds state-tracking complexity. Token-safety + convergence are proven (test pins partial-resume). | DONE (token-safe; cursor won't-fix) | RESOLVED |

Classical homographs و°‘ن¸»/é€‰ن¸¾ stay excluded. Still a finite denylist (richer era-lexicon / model-assisted check remains a future option if domains widen) but materially broader + still false-positive-safe. | DONE (denylist broadened; lexicon upgrade still optional) | RESOLVED |
| LE-059 | 2026-06-01 lore-enrichment C1 token metering | **P2/P3 post-call token reconcile + real embed usage.** âœ… **(a) DONE 2026-06-01:** `assembly.build_live_runner` now wires the `UsageMeter` for ALL techniques (was P1-only) â€” P2 fabrication / P3 re-cook pre-charge in tokens (3000/4500) and use the same metered seams, so a multi-pass gap's REAL token spend is captured (`UsageMeter` accumulates every call) + reconciled per gap, like P1; runner reconcile is technique-agnostic (test pins a fabrication-magnitude gap reconciling to real tokens). **(b) âœ… DONE 2026-06-04:** provider-registry `parseOpenAIEmbeddingResponse` now surfaces the upstream `usage.prompt_tokens` on `EmbedResult.PromptTokens` (json `prompt_tokens`, omitempty â†’ wire-compatible; +2 Go tests); the `/internal/embed` handler returns `result` directly so it flows automatically. lore-enrichment `EmbedResult.prompt_tokens` + the embed seam meters on the REAL count when present, **falling back to the char-estimate only when the provider reports none** (Ollama / a too-old provider-registry) â†’ no regression, never metered as 0 (+2 py tests: real-count vs fallback). **ADDITIVE + backward-compatible** (Go/Python both ignore unknown fields). **Op:** takes effect live only after a provider-registry rebuild (shared daemon â€” clobber-prone per LE-061; until then lore-enrichment gracefully estimates). **âœ… LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-06-04 (D-LE059B):** rebuilt+recreated provider-registry; `/internal/embed` (test-user bge-m3) â†’ 200, dim 1024, embeddings present; the parse reads LM Studio's `usage` block but **LM Studio reports `prompt_tokens: 0` for embeddings** (confirmed by hitting `:1234/v1/embeddings` directly) â†’ `omitempty` drops it â†’ lore-enrichment falls back to the char-estimate. So the full chain works as designed; the REAL-count path is exercised only by providers that report non-zero embed usage (e.g. real OpenAI) â€” the Go unit test pins that parse. No regression on the local stack. /review-impl added a `>0` guard so a negative count can never reduce metered spend. **(c) STILL OPEN (LOW):** the `UsageMeter` per-gap delta assumes sequential gaps â€” revisit only if gap processing is parallelized. | (c) only if gaps parallelized | LOW |
| ~~LE-060~~ | 2026-06-01 lore-enrichment C3 auto-reject (F-C12-1 + /review-impl) | âœ… **RESOLVED 2026-06-01.** `app/verify/canon_lookup.py extract_canon_terms` now segments CJK canon with **jieba POS-tagging** (`jieba>=0.42`, requirements.txt; lazily imported so its dict loads only when a canon term is actually extracted), keeping **proper-noun tags only** (ns/nr/nt/nzâ€¦) and dropping generic nouns/verbs/adjectives â€” consistent with the Latin Capitalized rule, so the C3 false-positive-safety holds (a benign common-word + negation can't fire a contradiction). Test pins proper nouns kept (è“¬èگٹ/وک†ن»‘/ن»™ه±±) + generics dropped (هœ°و–¹/ن»™ن؛؛/ن؟®éپ“). /review-impl pinned the graceful-degrade contract (jieba absent â†’ extract raises â†’ verifier `verify_degraded`, no false-green). **Residuals (accepted, LOW):** (a) tag-set couples to jieba's version â€” a tag change drops a term â†’ under-fires (safe), guarded by the LE-060 test; (b) Latin NER not added (Capitalized heuristic retained); (c) prod read-only-rootfs would force a jieba in-memory dict rebuild per start. ~~**âڑ ï¸ڈ Images need rebuild to ship jieba**~~ **âœ… images rebuilt + live-proven 2026-06-02** (jieba loads in-container; full e2e enrich ran through canon-verify with no `verify_degraded`). | DONE (jieba; NER + read-only-fs are LOW residuals) | RESOLVED |
| ~~LE-061~~ | 2026-06-02 live-e2e root-cause | âœ… **RESOLVED 2026-06-02** â€” freshness guard hardened: added a provider-registry `/internal/embed` route-presence probe (catches a fully-stale binary missing the route) AND a **UNSTAMPED** warning when a first-party image has no `git_sha` label (surfaces the degraded tier-1-only detection that let this slip); `scripts/check_stack_freshness.py` + `drift_note()` + 2 new unit tests. provider-registry itself was rebuilt + SHA-stamped. (Behavioural `/v1` staleness is now caught by tier-2 SHA-drift once stamped.) ORIGINAL: **provider-registry was a STALE, unstamped image** (`org.loreweave.git_sha` label empty â†’ never built via `build-stack.sh`); its binary predated the embed `/v1`-normalization (`internal/provider/embed.go` `TrimSuffix(base,"/v1")`). A `user_model` whose lm_studio credential `endpoint_base_url` ends in `/v1` â†’ the stale binary built `â€¦/v1/v1/embeddings` â†’ LM Studio returns **200 with `{error}` and empty `data`** â†’ provider-registry maps it to 502 `EMBED_PROVIDER_FAILED "no embedding data in response"` â€” for ALL embed consumers (lore-enrichment retrieval, knowledge-service, etc.) on a `/v1`-suffixed credential. **âœ… FIXED 2026-06-02 by rebuilding+recreating provider-registry** (source already strips `/v1`; test-user embed â†’ dim=1024 after). **RESIDUAL (the real deferred work):** the freshness guard (`scripts/check_stack_freshness.py`) did NOT catch this â€” provider-registry is NOT in the route-probe list AND its git_sha label was empty so tier-2 SHA-drift couldn't fire either. Add a provider-registry probe (e.g. a representative `/internal/embed` reachability check) + ensure every service image is SHA-stamped at build. | DONE (guard hardened; pr rebuilt) | RESOLVED |
| ~~LE-062~~ | 2026-06-02 live-e2e ops | âœ… **RESOLVED 2026-06-02** â€” replaced the inherited HTTP `/health` probe (meaningless for a headless consumer) with a **background-heartbeat liveness**: `app/worker/heartbeat.py` (a bg asyncio task touches a heartbeat file every 5s â€” stays fresh during long generations since the event loop is alive, goes stale only on a real hang/crash) + `app/worker/healthcheck.py` (`python -m app.worker.healthcheck` exits 0/1) + compose `healthcheck` override on the worker block + 7 unit tests. Wired in `app/worker/__main__.py` (both the active and consumer-disabled branches). ORIGINAL: **`lore-enrichment-worker` healthcheck is a permanent false-negative.** The compose `lore-enrichment-worker` block inherits the service's HTTP healthcheck (`urlopen http://127.0.0.1:8093/health`) but the worker is a headless Redis-stream consumer (`python -m app.worker`) with NO HTTP server â†’ it shows `unhealthy` forever despite functioning correctly (consumer starts + drains the resume stream â€” live-proven). Fix: replace with a consumer-liveness check (heartbeat key/file, or `pgrep -f app.worker`) or drop the healthcheck for the worker block. Non-blocking (does not stop consumption), purely a misleading status. | DONE (heartbeat liveness) | RESOLVED |
| ~~LE-063~~ | 2026-06-02 live-e2e (promote chain) | âœ… **RESOLVED 2026-06-02** â€” `ProposalsRepo.approve()` (new) walks `proposed â†’ author_reviewing â†’ approved` in one call (each hop DAG- + DB-trigger-legal, H0 unchanged), and the `approve` endpoint now calls it; this restores the documented "proposed/reviewing â†’ approved" contract so a freshly-created proposal can be approved â†’ promoted via the API alone (no DB poke). +1 unit test (`test_approve_from_proposed_walks_through_reviewing`). A distinct FE-facing "begin-review" toggle remains optional. ORIGINAL: **Proposal lifecycle API cannot promote a fresh `proposed` proposal.** The DAG (`app/services/review.py LEGAL_TRANSITIONS`) is `proposed â†’ author_reviewing â†’ approved â†’ promoted`, but the API (`app/api/proposals.py`) exposes only `approve` (sets `approved` directly), `reject`, `edit` (no status change), `promote`, `retract`, `write-back` â€” there is **no endpoint for `proposed â†’ author_reviewing`**. So `approve` from the initial `proposed` state 409s (`illegal transition proposed â†’ approved`), making `promote` unreachable through the API alone. The 2026-06-02 e2e worked around it with a one-row DB `UPDATE â€¦ author_reviewing` (the H0 trigger permits it) before calling the real `approve`+`promote` API. Fix: add a `begin-review` endpoint (`proposed â†’ author_reviewing`), OR have `approve` walk `proposed â†’ author_reviewing â†’ approved` in one step. The FE review flow presumably needs this too. | DONE (approve auto-walks) | RESOLVED |

| ~~D-COMPOSE-KIND-HARDCODE-INTERIM~~ | 2026-06-04 Compose live-smoke | âœ… **RESOLVED 2026-06-04 (epic E2).** Deleted `_ENRICH_TO_GLOSSARY_KIND`, `_DEFAULT_GLOSSARY_KIND`, `_glossary_kind_code`, the reactive `_write_glossary_anchor`/`UnplaceableEntityError` fallback from `app/services/writeback.py`; promote sends `proposal.entity_kind` RAW. | ~~D-KNOWLEDGE-ENRICHED-WRITEBACK-IDEMPOTENCY~~ | 2026-06-04 E2 live-smoke | âœ… **RESOLVED 2026-06-04.** (Investigation corrected the original wording: `internal_enrichment.py` was ALREADY a `MERGE`, never a raw CREATE.) The real defect: the anchor MERGEd on `(user_id, glossary_entity_id)` while `ON CREATE SET e.id = canon_id` (a name+kind hash) â€” so a NEW glossary_entity_id mapping to an existing `canon_id` (glossary entity deleted+recreated same name, or rename) MISSED the node and tripped `UNIQUE(:Entity.id)` â†’ 500. This also mismatched the canonical glossaryâ†’KG sync, which MERGEs `(:Entity {id})`. **Fix:** the write-back now MERGEs on the canonical `id` (matching the sync), frees a stale `glossary_entity_id` from any other node first (null-before-claim, mirroring `entities.py`), and ON MATCH only coalesces the anchor (H0 canon untouched). Facts re-key to the anchor by `id`. **Live-proven** (`scripts/smoke_writeback_idempotency.py`, rebuilt knowledge-service): two write-backs with DIFFERENT glossary_entity_ids for the same canonical entity â†’ both 200 + re-promote 200; cypher confirms a SINGLE `:Entity` node (no duplicate id); test nodes cleaned. | DONE | RESOLVED |
| ~~D-GLOSSARY-KIND-ALIASES~~ | 2026-06-04 Compose live-smoke (root cause) | âœ… **EPIC CLOSED 2026-06-04 (E1 + E2 + E3 all DONE + live-proven).** Root cause: glossary `extract-entities` silently SKIPPED an unknown `kind_code`. **E1 (glossary BE):** alias table + `unknown` kind + `source_kind_code` + alias-aware resolution-never-skip + 4 review endpoints w/ attr re-key. **E2 (lore-enrichment):** aliases seeded + hardcode deleted. **E3 (glossary FE):** unknown-kind review GUI (queue + merge-as-alias / create-kind / reassign) â€” `/review-impl` fixed 5 + 1 smoke-found HIGH (name-loss on reassign â†’ `createKind` seeds a `name` attr + `rekeyEntityToKind` `name`â†”`term` mapping). Live-proven via `smoke_alias_selfcode_e2e.py`. | DONE | RESOLVED |
| D-GLOSSARY-UNKNOWN-BLAST-RADIUS | 2026-06-04 E1 /review-impl #2/#3 | **Accepted/documented (the explicit "never drop" design).** The unknown-bucket fallback in `extract-entities` applies to ALL callers incl. knowledge-service's extraction pipeline â€” kinds it emits that glossary lacks now park under `unknown` (were skipped). Review queue is the triage. If the pipeline ever emits noise kinds they accumulate (gate behind a request flag if it bites). Also: same-name + different-bogus-kind entities collapse into one `unknown` row (findEntityByNameOrAlias keys on the single unknown kind; only the first source_kind_code kept) â€” rare; revisit if observed. | Only if the pipeline floods unknown / a same-name collapse is observed | LOW |
| ~~D-GLOSSARY-KIND-ALIAS-GOTEST~~ | 2026-06-04 E1 verification | âœ… **RESOLVED 2026-06-04.** Added `internal/api/kind_aliases_test.go` (gated on GLOSSARY_TEST_DB_URL): 4 tests â€” bogus kind â†’ parked under `unknown` + `source_kind_code`; seeded alias `genericâ†’terminology` resolves; merge reassigns + preserves the name across the `name`â†’`term` display mapping (#6); self-code merge skips the redundant alias but reassigns + dead-alias still 409 (#2-glossary); deleteKind blocks active but purges soft-deleted (#3). All PASS against the `glossary_test` DB; idempotent via a per-test fixture reset. (A `NOT EXISTS` guard on `rekeyEntityToKind` for non-triage reassign remains a tiny latent item, folded into normal review.) | DONE | RESOLVED |
| ~~D-GLOSSARY-DELETEKIND-SOFTCOUNT~~ | 2026-06-04 E3 live-smoke | âœ… **RESOLVED 2026-06-04.** Investigation found the naive "add `deleted_at IS NULL`" fix would turn the clean 409 into an FK-violation 500 (the `glossary_entities.kind_id` FK has NO `ON DELETE CASCADE`, so soft-deleted rows still physically block the kind delete). **Proper fix:** `deleteKind` now guards on ACTIVE entities only (409 if any) and, inside a tx, PURGES the kind's soft-deleted entities (their children cascade) before deleting the kind (whose attr_defs + aliases cascade) â€” so an effectively-empty kind is deletable. Covered by the gated Go test. | DONE | RESOLVED |

Root: the short-description backfill runs in a main.go goroutine that the in-process test server doesn't start, and the seed/book state in the shared test DB is stale. Fix: make those two tests self-seed their short_description + book instead of relying on backfilled/shared state (or reset the test DB in CI). | Next glossary test-hardening | LOW |
| ~~LE-068~~ | 2026-06-03 enrichment closure (S7) | âœ… **RESOLVED 2026-06-04 â€” real browser-layer e2e of the C3 GUI run + green.** The earlier "blockers" were partly wrong: a FULL Playwright harness already exists (`frontend/tests/e2e/` â€” page objects, helpers, specs, `LoginPage`, `TEST_USER`), and `npx playwright test` uses node-Playwright's OWN bundled chromium (installed) â€” the "shared Chrome lock" was the **MCP** browser, not this. The only real gap was serving THIS worktree's FE; solved by running its **vite dev on :5273** (proxies `/v1`â†’the live gateway :3123). Added `pages/EnrichmentTab.ts` + `specs/enrichment-profile.spec.ts`: **2 tests PASS** (10.8s) against the live stack â€” (1) loginâ†’gatewayâ†’FEâ†’Enrichmentâ†’**Profile tab loads the seeded Fengshen profile** (GET `/books/{id}/profile`, owner-checked via book-service) with suggest disabled + save visible; (2) gaps **detect shows NO "extract first" notice** on the extracted demo book (C2 signal wired correctly). > **âœ… RESOLVED 2026-06-03 (was out-of-scope, fixed on request):** 9 stale failures in `knowledge/BuildGraphDialog.test.tsx`. Root cause was NOT dep-drift â€” commit `0bf049cd` switched the LLM `<select>` to send `user_model_id` (`value={m.user_model_id}`) but didn't update these tests, which still drove it with the old `provider_model_name` (`'gpt-5'`, not a valid option) â†’ the select stayed empty â†’ the estimate query never enabled â†’ mocks never called. They went unnoticed because this worktree never ran FE tests (no `node_modules`). **Fixed (test-only):** drive the select with `'m1'` (the `user_model_id`) + update the `llm_model` assertions. Full FE suite now 100 files / 813 tests green. Dossier S5.

| 046 | 2026-05-31 session 100 moisture-transport model | **Fixed.** Rewrote `climate.rs::moisture_field` from **averaging** upwind neighbours to **MAX best-path** downwind-directed multi-source transport (`moisture[i] = max over upwind nbrs of moisture[nb] âˆ’ land_leak âˆ’ OROGRAPHICآ·climb`; windward-edge = off-map upwind ocean). MAX lets moisture reach inland along the wettest route from any upwind sea (multi-directional within the upwind cone) while staying wind-aware (offshore coasts dry, range rain-shadows persist). Result (seed-7 mega): interiors greener, C-group nearly doubled in the full-gradient case (equatorial spread=1: 2.2â†’3.9 %, `Plain` 55â†’98), Desert preserved 30.7 % at spread=0; bonus â€” maritime cooling raised Tundra/Polar (126â†’375 / 5.7â†’11 %). Tests: `moisture_takes_wettest_upwind_path` (MAX-vs-AVG discriminator), `rain_shadow_follows_the_wind` preserved. Full lib 399 green. Residual (abundant *literal* Temperate plains) split to optional **#047 (8-zone mapping)**. Commit: (this session). |
| 045 | 2026-05-31 session 100 Kأ¶ppen v2 seasonality | **Fixed.** Replaced the linear insolation `lerp(28,âˆ’15,lat_dist)` with a **cosine** curve (`insolation_temp`: warms mid-lat ~6.5â†’15 آ°C at 45آ°) and rewrote `seasonal_amp` to be **continentality-gated** (`AMP_EQ + (AMP_MARITIME=4 + AMP_CONT_GAIN=24آ·cont)آ·lat_dist`) so maritime coasts stay low-amplitude at all latitudes. Result (seed-7 mega): Desert preserved 33.5 % at spread=0 (Kأ¶ppen win intact); **Tundra opened 0â†’126** + Polar/Boreal gradient at spread=1; the temperate C-band is now reachable (`Plain` 0â†’55 with Equatorial orientation). Tests: `insolation_warms_midlatitudes`, `maritime_stays_low_amplitude`, `v2_seasonality_opens_temperate_and_polar` (drives the exact build composition). Full lib 398 green. The *residual* â€” Temperate stays only 1â€“2 % because it's a wet-mild maritime climate the single-wind moisture march under-produces â€” is split out as new **#046 (moisture-transport model)**, the chosen next step. Commit: (this session). |
| 038 | 2026-05-24 V1 tilemap-viewer rescope (same session it was created) | HoMM3-bundle Flux1-dev placeholders replaced with first-party algorithm-generated foundation tiles (`frontend-game/scripts/gen-foundation-tiles.py`). No third-party model output baked in; ship-anywhere license. Spec rescope: `docs/specs/2026-05-24-v1-tilemap-viewer-rescope.md`. |
| 024 | 2026-05-24 v2.1e ship (geo-generator-amaw branch) | W9 shipped as amplitude-gated RGB shading (zone relief auto-scales intensity 0â†’1 across Plainsâ†’Mountains; آ±15% RGB modulation). Hypothesis "naive RGB regressed because eval framework had a problem" CONFIRMED: under v4.3 ecotone-aware eval, naive RGB amplitude-gated produces only âˆ’0.24 mean (neutral, 0 hard regressions) vs the v2.1b attempt's hard regression. Visual benefit currently limited (visible only on Ice zones where white amplifies modulation) but ships as foundation for future 3D map rendering. HSL approach not needed. True slope-aware hillshade (spec option B) still deferred to v3+. |
| 027 | 2026-05-22 tilemap test hardening | Added `a_bridge_mid_river_resets_the_periodic_ford_counter` (river_placer.rs): a 30أ—3 straight river with a road at x=6; asserts the bridge resets the shared `since_crossing` counter, so the periodic ford lands at x=18 (12 carves after the bridge) â€” NOT x=12 (which would mean the bridge didn't reset). Closes the interleaving coverage gap between `a_long_river_gets_periodic_fords` (pure every-Nth) and `a_river_crossing_a_road...` (pure bridge). Test-only; no output change. |
| 026 | 2026-05-22 tilemap river barrier reorder | Root cause refined: **erosion** (not fill) fragments the passable region, and the river's Mountain/Lake sources/sinks required erosion first â€” so the river was structurally stuck threading post-erosion narrow channels (forded most tiles). Fix: split `ObstaclePlacer` into `ObstacleSourcePlacer` (places Mountain/Lake markers on Open tiles **pre-erosion**, dual-gated per-zone + map-wide) â†’ `RiverPlacer` (carves the wide-open zone) â†’ `ObstacleFillPlacer` (erode + fill the rest post-river, skipping river Water; erosion also skips Water fords). Golden fixture river ford ratio: **~0.77 crossings â†’ 0.06 fords** (AC-2 gates < 0.25). Cost tradeoff accepted: continent 6.46 s â†’ 10.56 s (river now carves a real barrier). Strict dual `would_seal_a_gap` gate unchanged. `/review-impl` caught a HIGH (source placer needed the map-wide gate arm, not per-zone only) â€” fixed + regression-tested. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-22-tilemap-river-barrier-reorder.md`](../specs/2026-05-22-tilemap-river-barrier-reorder.md). |
| 029 | 2026-05-21 tilemap perf fix | `place_and_connect_object` refactored to **score-first, validate-on-demand**: cheap-filter + score all candidates (no flood fill), sort by `(score desc, flat asc)`, walk in best-first order and validate (`would_seal_a_gap` + `find_access_path`) lazily. The first sorted candidate that passes IS the winner (spec آ§4 proof: `first âˆˆ V` of sorted `S` == `argmax_{v âˆˆ V} (score, -flat)`). Naive kept under `#[cfg(test)]` as oracle. **Measurement: `treasure_placer` 973.376 s â†’ 2.944 s (330أ—); continent total 992.963 s â†’ 21.541 s (46أ—).** Golden test passes byte-exact â€” no rebaseline. AC-1 oracle: 405 agreements across 3 fixtures أ— 3 templates أ— 3 optimisations أ— 3 min-distances أ— 5 sequential placements. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-21-tilemap-place-and-connect-perf.md`](../specs/2026-05-21-tilemap-place-and-connect-perf.md). |
| 018 | 2026-05-20 tilemap perf fix | `penrose::nearest_vertex` replaced with a bucket-based spiral search (`build_vertex_buckets` + `nearest_vertex_bucketed` over the new `UniformBuckets<Vec2>` helper in `engine/placement/spatial.rs`). Bit-exact equivalent to the prior linear scan (AC-2 oracle: 800 random queries أ— 4 vertex-field configurations incl. 1500-vertex dense; AC-3 cross-bucket tie-break by lowest index). Per-stage continent measurement: `place_zones` (Penrose + fractalize) dropped to **0.110 s** at 256آ² â€” well under the TMP_002 آ§7 <500 ms budget. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md`](../specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md). |
| 016 | 2026-05-20 tilemap perf fix | `fractalize::scatter_and_connect` replaced with a bucket-based "any within radius" rewrite using the same `UniformBuckets<TileCoord>` helper â€” 3أ—3 ring neighbourhood query (sufficiency proven: `bucket_size = ceil(sqrt(coverage_sq))` â‡’ ring-2 min distآ² â‰¥ 49 > 36 surface / 25 > 16 sea). Bit-exact equivalent to the prior `nearest_cleared_dist_sq` linear scan (AC-1 oracle: 96آ² zone أ— 5 seeds أ— 2 spans). The naive impl is kept under `#[cfg(test)]` as the oracle. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md`](../specs/2026-05-20-tilemap-perf-fractalize-penrose.md). |
| 022 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase E | Resolved by the Phase-E `RiverPlacer` design (spec D-Q5/D-Q6): the `anchor` representative point **suffices** â€” no footprint extent needed. River sources are one lowest-flat-index `Mountain` obstacle anchor per mountain-bearing zone; sinks are the `Lake` obstacle anchors âˆھ `Sea`-zone tiles. `search_path` routes to/from the anchor tile, which is added to the river search area. No additive `TilemapObjectPlacement` footprint field was required. |
| 019 | 2026-05-18 tilemap Phase D | Re-validated when ConnectionsPlacer landed. `seal_borders` (آ§3.1/آ§9) seals inter-zone borders but is not a complete wall â€” passage corridors stay `Walkable` and gap-critical tiles stay `Open`. The per-zone `would_seal_a_gap` is sound regardless: every tile belongs to exactly one zone, and every passability-removing mutation (placer objects, monoliths, guards, ferries, border seals) targets an `Open` tile that is zone-interior â€” so removing a zone-X tile can never split zone Y. Border-seal completeness is irrelevant to correctness; the original "border thickness" framing was the wrong lens. |
| 013 | 2026-05-17 GEO Phase 2 | Connectivity-aware `choose_sea_level` â€” binary-searches `sea_level` so the largest land component â‰ˆ the profile target (`maxcomp(L(s))` is monotone non-increasing in `s`), plus a continental base dome for the high-land `Inland` profile (a blob-only heightmap cannot form a connected 70% landmass). Realised land fraction is now within آ±0.08 of target, or a documented 8192 best-effort floor (آ±0.18). Verified by `land_fraction_near_target` (16-seed sweep أ— 4 profiles). |
| 012 | 2026-05-16 tilemap fix task | Added `TestStreamOpenAICompat_MultipleToolCalls` â€” two tool calls streamed with interleaved/non-monotonic `tool_calls[].index` (0,1,1,0); asserts `Index` is verbatim from the provider and per-index reassembly stays separate. |
| 011 | 2026-05-16 tilemap fix task | Dropped `nullable: true` from openapi `ChatStreamRequest.tool_choice` â€” the field is now `description` + `oneOf` only (idiomatic OpenAPI 3.0; still optional via not-in-`required`). No runtime change; SDKs already model it optional. |

| 043 | 2026-05-30 C4 independent-adversary WARN-1 | **Manual single-entity name-fill does not propagate to Neo4j.** glossary `createEntity` emits an empty-name event (consumer skips it); the `PATCH /entities/{id}/attributes/{attr_value_id}` path (`attribute_handler.go patchAttributeValue`) that fills the name never calls `emitEntityUpdated`, so a manually-authored entity reaches Neo4j only when an unrelated `patchEntity` (status/tags/pin) fires. The enrichment DEMO path (extract-entities bulk, `extraction_handler.go`) emits the name inline and IS live-smoke-confirmed â€” unaffected. Fix: add `s.emitEntityUpdated(ctx, entityID, "updated")` at the end of the setClauses block in `services/glossary-service/internal/api/attribute_handler.go` (~:159), mirroring `patchEntity`. | Platform completeness â€” when manual single-entity authoring needs KG propagation | MED |
